page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is a compute-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 176 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors running at 2.7 GHz, mapped to 88 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 352 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and PassMark CPU suite both rank in the top 10%, reflecting high parallel processing capabilities. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 11,970.72 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 458.9 GB/sec. The instance also demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, achieving 164.37 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, and static web serving throughput of 126.23 GB/sec. Single-core performance, however, remains in the average tier across stress-ng and Geekbench tests.

Given its high density of dedicated vCPUs relative to memory, the c3-highcpu-176 offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that require massive parallelization without the overhead of excessive RAM. The presence of AMX instructions enables strong CPU-based LLM inference performance, particularly in prompt processing and medium-model text generation. This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput web serving, large-scale database operations, and parallel scientific simulations.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 176 dedicated vCPUs and 352 GB of RAM, powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 2.7 GHz. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, it relies on its 88 physical cores and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX to deliver top-tier multi-core performance. Benchmark data highlights exceptional multi-threaded capabilities, with top 10% rankings in stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and memory bandwidth tests. It achieves 164.37 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 126.23 GB/sec in static web serving. This instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy, parallelized tasks, making it ideal for large-scale database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server delivering exceptional multi-core processing, high memory bandwidth, and advanced matrix acceleration for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 176 dedicated vCPUs (88 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 2.7 GHz.
352 GB of system memory providing a balanced 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
Optimized for multi-threaded software compilation, Redis databases, and CPU-based LLM inference.
